title: [CI:DOCS] troubleshooting: mention machinectl and systemd-run
body:
Signed-off-by: Erik Sjölund <erik.sjolund@gmail.com>

I'm adding this PR as a sketch, because I haven't checked the details yet.
It might very well be that systemd version 248 is needed instead of systemd version 249.

This PR
https://github.com/systemd/systemd/pull/17967
gives an indication that the requirement is systemd v248.


<!--

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name.  Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *eriksjolund*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
LGTM once @Luap99 's comment is agreed upon.

i removed the two occurrences of
_(__systemd__ version >= 249 is required)_
as I haven't investigated exactly which versions are required.

The PR currently doesn't touch this text
```
* Before invoking Podman command create a valid login session for your rootless user using `loginctl enable-linger <username>`
```
Maybe a reboot is required afterwards, or maybe some `sudo systemctl restart` command? Anyway, that could be addressed in another PR in the future.
